The epidermis shows mild atypia. The dermis contains a dense infiltrate of atypical melanocytes with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli.

Melanoma.

The epidermis shows mild atypia. The dermis contains a haphazardly arranged collection of melanocytes with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli. These features are suggestive of melanoma.

Melanoma.

The image shows dermal melanocytes. The arrows point to these melanocytes. The lesion is composed of nests of melanocytes.

Melanoma.

The image shows a dermal proliferation of melanocytes. There is a prominent atypical melanocytic proliferation with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli.

Melanoma.
Melanoma. The epidermis shows irregular nests of melanocytes with atypical nuclei and prominent nucleoli. There is evidence of dermal invasion.

The epidermis shows mild atypia. There is a dermal component with prominent lymphoid aggregates.

Melanoma.

The biopsy shows a dense proliferation of atypical melanocytes with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli, infiltrating the dermis. This is consistent with melanoma.
Melanoma.

The epidermis shows a regular pattern of nests of melanocytes with peripheral palisading. The dermis is unremarkable.
Melanoma.
(c) The image shows a dermal proliferation of atypical melanocytes with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli. Diagnosis: Melanoma.
Basal cell hyperplasia and increased melanin pigment are present. The diagnosis is MELANOMA.
